data.title: Asana rises after rating upgrade at Citi
data.description:
Citi upgraded Asana's (ASAN) rating to Buy/High Risk from Neutral/High Risk while maintaining its $16 price target on the stock.

Shares of Asana, which operates a work management software platform, rose about 4% premarket on Thursday.

"We see an opportunity for new leadership to improve S&M [Sales & Marketing] spend efficiency from bottom quartile closer to the median based on delivered results from both new CEO Rogers and CFO Parekh in prior roles, helping support potential top-line reacceleration and further margin improvements," said analysts led by Steven Enders.

The analysts see additive growth potential from AI Studio, which they conservatively estimate will drive +1 point of growth to Annual Recurring Revenue, or ARR, in fiscal year 2027. They also see  a potential acceleration along with adding an AI consumption element to the model, which should prove helpful in combating pessimism around the seat-based model/other generative AI, or GenAI, tooling fears.

"While early, our conversations with implementation partners suggest strong initial interest in AI Studio, positive feedback on delivered ROI, and a differentiated offering," said Enders and his team.

data.title: Talen Energy to buy PJM gas assets from Energy Capital Partners in $3.45B deal
data.description:
Talen Energy (TLN) said Thursday it agreed to acquire the Waterford Energy Center and Darby Generating Station in Ohio and the Lawrenceburg Power Plant in Indiana from Energy Capital Partners, adding ~2.6 GW of natural gas generation capacity to its portfolio, for $3.45B, consisting of $2.55B in cash and $900M in stock.

The 1,218 MW Lawrenceburg and 869 MW Waterford facilities are modern combined cycle gas turbines that add both baseload generation and cash flow diversification, and the 480 MW Darby facility operates as a peaking unit and can provide attractive commercial flexibility, the company said.

Talen (TLN) said the acquisition will substantially expand its presence in the western PJM market and add additional efficient baseload generation assets to its fleet, and is expected to provide immediate and significant adjusted free cash flow per share accretion above 15% annually through 2030.

"When this transaction is complete, Talen will have approximately doubled its expected annual generation output inside of two years, meaningfully diversified our fleet, and materially increased our free cash flow per share," Talen Energy (TLN) President Terry Nutt said.

data.title: Disc Medicine and Sanofi reportedly face delays for FDA national priority review vouchers
data.description:
Two drugs developed by Disc Medicine (IRON) and Sanofi (SNY) are facing delays in their review timelines due to safety and efficacy concerns in the U.S. after being selected for the FDA Commissioner’s National Priority Review vouchers last year. Reuters reported on Thursday.

The regulators have delayed the review of Disc Medicine’s (IRON) rare disease therapy bitopertin by two months due to concerns over its potential for abuse and issues with trial data supporting the therapy, according to internal documents seen by the agency.

Meanwhile, Sanofi (SNY) is facing a delay of more than 12 months for its U.S. bid to expand the use of its type 1 diabetes therapy Tzield (teplizumab-mzwv) due to reports of adverse events, including seizures, blood clotting, and mortality.

Disc Medicine (IRON) and Sanofi (SNY) refused to respond to Reuters’ requests for details on the reviews.

The same documents indicated that the FDA review of Eli Lilly's (LLY) weight-loss pill, orforglipron, which was also picked for a National Priority Review voucher last year, is now expected to be completed by Apr. 10. This week, LLY CFO Lucas Montarce said that the Indiana-based pharma giant remains on track for a potential FDA nod for orforglipron in Q2 2026.

The FDA unveiled a pilot program in June 2025 to offer the Commissioner’s National Priority Review vouchers to companies aligned with U.S. national interests, aiming to expedite the review of their experimental therapies from 10–12 months to just 1–2 months.

Companies including Disc Medicine (IRON), Sanofi (SNY), Achieve Life Sciences (ACHV), and Revolution Medicines (RVMD) were among the first nine recipients of the vouchers.

data.title: Lucara raises private placement to C$165M
data.description:
• Lucara Diamond (LUC:CA) (LUCRF) raised non-brokered private placement to 1.03B common shares at a price of $0.16 per common share for gross proceeds of C$165M.
  • All dollar amounts are reflected in Canadian dollars unless otherwise stated. 
  • Net proceeds will be used to advance the Karowe Underground Project, including for shaft equipping, conveyance commissioning, and lateral development; extraction and drill horizon development; and general working capital and corporate purposes.

data.title: Reactions to Toyota Motor's higher buyout offer for Toyota Industries
data.description:
Toyota Motor (TM) raised the buyout offer to minority shareholders of Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF) after investor pushback to 18,800 yen per share, from 16,300 yen apiece.

The deal is seen by analysts as being as much about tightening control and simplifying the Toyota (TM) group’s structure as it is about economics, given Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF)  role as the conglomerate’s original core company and a key supplier.

"We think the increase in the  price had been somewhat anticipated by the equity market, so it did not come as a surprise. However, the fact that the offer is not at a discount should improve the likelihood of completion. There may be a potential for a further increase in the TOB price, but overall, we view the removal of downward pressure on Toyota’s share price positively," updated analyst Shiro Sakamaki. Bof hiked its price objective on Toyota Motor (TM) to ¥4,000 from ¥3,500 to ¥4,000 (ADR PO raised to $271.68 from $230.93). The firm said it increased the premium by considering currency trends and the overall rise in the equity market valuation, as well as expectations for new models like the RAV4.

Activist investors Elliott Investment Management L.P. and Elliott Advisors (UK), which advise funds that together have a significant ownership stake in Toyota Industries, issued the following statement on the development:

"Although the revised tender offer price of ¥18,800 per share acknowledges the inadequacy of the original transaction terms, the new tender offer price continues to very substantially undervalue Toyota Industries."

Of note, Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF) is Japan’s largest forklift producer and a major materials-handling player, and it also makes engines and components for key Toyota-branded vehicles such as the RAV4, Corolla, Crown, and Land Cruiser. Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF) is still a major shareholder in Toyota Motor (around 8% of its shares), while Toyota Motor (TM) in turn owns roughly a quarter of Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF), which created a classic cross-shareholding loop used for decades as a defense against hostile takeovers.

Shares of Toyota Motor (TM) were up 3.4% in premarket trading in the U.S. Toyota Industries (TYIDY) (TYIDF) jumped 6.2% in Tokyo trading.

data.title: Carney in China: Canada looks to reset bilateral ties, Beijing calls visit 'pivotal'
data.description:
Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney is in China for a four-day visit, aiming to diversify trade and improve bilateral ties that had soured over tariffs that were imposed by his predecessor, Justin Trudeau.

This marks the first China visit by a Canadian prime minister since 2017. Carney is scheduled to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping on Friday. 

Carney met with Chinese Premier Li Qiang on Thursday and discussed changes in the global economy, including challenges impacting international trade and global supply chains.

"To this end, they welcomed a series of memorandums of understanding across energy, combatting crime, modern wood construction, culture, and food safety and plant and animal health that provide the foundation for a new strategic partnership," a statement from Carney's office read.

The countries agreed to cooperate in areas including clean energy, oil and gas resource development, and emission reduction, CBC reported. So far, there has been no breakthrough on tariffs.

"We will double non-U.S. trade over the next 10 years," said Canadian Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand, who is also in Beijing. "That means we are cognizant of the fact that the global economic environment has fundamentally changed and that Canada must diversify its trading partners."

Wang Yi, China's foreign minister, said Carney's visit is of "pivotal and symbolic significance" to bilateral relations, and Beijing is willing to strengthen communication with Canada and "eliminate interference."

-- The Associated Press contributed to this report.

data.title: Parsons acquires Altamira Technologies for $375M
data.description:
• Parsons (PSN) has acquired Northern Virginia-based Altamira Technologies in a transaction valued at about $375 million.
  • Altamira enhances Parsons’ defense and intelligence portfolio by delivering advanced analytics, signals intelligence (SIGINT), cyber, missile warning, and space capabilities, complementing the company’s strengths in all‑domain technology integration and Indo‑Pacific operations, and expanding with Intelligence Community (IC) customers, the company said.
  • Parsons paid $330 million in cash at closing for Altamira, with an additional $45 million cash earnout payable in Q1 2027 if certain EBITDA targets are met during 2026.
  • The base purchase price implies a 12.8x multiple on the anticipated Altamira 2026 EBITDA contribution before considering any revenue or cost synergies.
  • Parsons estimates Altamira will generate over $200 million of revenue in 2026 and that the transaction will be accretive to Parsons’ fiscal year 2026 revenue growth rate, adjusted EBITDA margin, and adjusted earnings per share. 
  • PSN +4.39% premarket to $73.46.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump admin rolls back $2B in mental health, addiction grant cuts—report
data.description:
• The Trump administration is rolling back $2B in cuts to mental health and addiction programs, NPR reported, citing a Trump administration official.
  • Letters restoring the funding are expected to be sent in the coming days. More than 2,000 organizations and grant recipients are being notified as quickly as possible, the source said.
  • Letters terminating the funding were sent late Tuesday, triggering widespread concern across the public health system. Following political backlash from both Republicans and Democrats, the Department of Health and Human Services reversed course and will restore the grant funding.

data.title: Apogee declares $0.27 dividend
data.description:
• Apogee (APOG) declares $0.27/share quarterly dividend, 3.8% increase from prior dividend of $0.26.
  • Forward yield 3.04%
  • Payable Feb. 18; for shareholders of record Feb. 3; ex-div Feb. 3.
  • See APOG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Valkea raises C$2.5M privately
data.description:
• Valkea Resources (OZBKF) closed the non-brokered private placement (the "Financing") for gross proceeds of C$2.5M.
  • A total of 10M  units were issued at a price of $0.25 per unit.
  • Each unit is comprised of one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant at an exercise price of C$0.35 for a period of 36 months.
  • The proceeds will be used for exploration of the company's flagship Paana project and working capital purposes.
